title: Tabby's Star: The Signal That Made Scientists Whisper 'Alien Megastructure'
hook: In 2015, a star over a thousand light years away started dimming in a way that broke every normal explanation astronomers had.
body: Stars dim a little when a planet passes in front of them, but always by a tiny, predictable, repeating amount. Tabby's Star dimmed by up to 22 percent, on no regular schedule at all. A planet couldn't do that; nothing planet-sized blocks that much light. For a brief moment, some researchers seriously floated the idea of an artificial structure built by an advanced civilization to harvest starlight. Follow-up studies since then point to something less dramatic but still strange: uneven clouds of dust orbiting the star. But the dust explanation doesn't cleanly account for every dip that's been recorded, which is why the star is still watched closely today.
cta: It's still the closest a mainstream science paper has come to seriously proposing aliens. Follow for more signals nobody's fully explained.
description: Why did Tabby's Star briefly have scientists talking about alien megastructures? The dimming pattern nobody can fully explain.
